meanings.definition: examine so as to determine accuracy, quality, or condition
make an examination or investigation
examples: check the time of the class

meanings.definition: be careful or certain to do something; make certain of something
examples: He verified that the valves were closed
See that the curtains are closed
control the quality of the product

meanings.definition: lessen the intensity of; temper; hold in restraint; hold or keep within limits
stop for a moment, as if out of uncertainty or caution
examples: She checked for an instant and missed a step

meanings.definition: put a check mark on or near or next to
examples: Please check each name on the list

meanings.definition: slow the growth or development of
examples: The brain damage will retard the child's language development

meanings.definition: be verified or confirmed; pass inspection
be compatible, similar or consistent; coincide in their characteristics
examples: The two stories don't agree in many details
The handwriting checks with the signature on the check
The suspect's fingerprints don't match those on the gun

meanings.definition: block or impede (a player from the opposing team) in ice hockey
develop (children's) behavior by instruction and practice; especially to teach self-control
consign for shipment on a vehicle
hand over something to somebody as for temporary safekeeping
examples: Check your coat at the door

meanings.definition: abandon the intended prey, turn, and pursue an inferior prey
stop in a chase especially when scent is lost
mark into squares or draw squares on; draw crossed lines on
decline to initiate betting
hold back, as of a danger or an enemy; check the expansion or influence of
examples: Check the growth of communism in South East Asia
Turn back the tide of communism

meanings.definition: place into check
write out a check on a bank account
find out, learn, or determine with certainty, usually by making an inquiry or other effort
examples: I want to see whether she speaks French
find out if he speaks Russian
Check whether the train leaves on time

meanings.definition: verify by consulting a source or authority
examples: check the spelling of this word

meanings.definition: arrest the motion (of something) abruptly
examples: He checked the flow of water by shutting off the main valve

meanings.definition: make cracks or chinks in
become fractured; break or crack on the surface only
examples: The glass cracked when it was heated

meanings.definition: patterned with alternating squares of color
